phenotypes.label: Urticaria/angioedema with unrelated conditions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are allergic skin reactions and swelling (urticaria, idiopathic urticaria, angioedema). These appear alongside unrelated disorders such as papillary thyroid carcinoma, malignant thyroid neoplasm, Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, and polycystic ovary syndrome, without a unifying clinical pathway.

phenotypes.label: Upper extremity fractures and injuries
phenotypes.description: Traumatic musculoskeletal pattern focused on upper-limb fractures (distal radius, ulnar styloid, humerus) and wrist/hand/elbow injuries with pain, swelling, stiffness, ecchymosis, and joint effusion. Co-occurrence with osteopenia and neurologic conditions (multiple sclerosis, seizures) points to fall-related and fragility injuries common in older adults with cognitive impairment.

phenotypes.label: Psychosis and mood disorder with suicidality
phenotypes.description: Severe psychiatric illness characterized by major depression and bipolar disorder with psychotic features (hallucinations, delusions), alongside generalized anxiety, insomnia, and mania/hypomania. Prominent suicidal and homicidal ideation/intent/behavior with intrusive or obsessional thoughts and personality/dissociative disorders indicate high acute safety risk. Injury and burn codes and extrapyramidal signs appear alongside, consistent with self-harm, trauma, and behavioral disturbance in this setting.

phenotypes.label: Breast cancer and complications
phenotypes.description: Breast malignancy spectrum including ductal carcinoma in situ and invasive breast carcinoma, with ER-positive tumors, palpable breast masses, nipple discharge, and related breast findings. Associated features include lymphadenopathy, lymphedema, decreased leukocyte counts with neutropenic fever, bone pain, and osteopenia.

phenotypes.label: Mixed neuro-ophthalmic and constitutional signs
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine constitutional symptoms (malaise/fatigue) and lymphoid enlargement (lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly) with eye findings such as dilated pupil and monocular diplopia. These span unrelated systems and do not form a single clinical syndrome in this cohort.

phenotypes.label: Paget’s disease of bone
phenotypes.description: This topic is driven by osteitis deformans (Paget’s disease of bone), a focal disorder of bone remodeling in older adults that presents with bone pain, deformity, and skeletal complications. Other listed conditions are incidental; the defining feature is a diagnosis of Paget’s disease.

phenotypes.label: Valvular heart disease with heart failure
phenotypes.description: Advanced structural and valvular heart disease with decompensated heart failure. Features include aortic and tricuspid valve regurgitation/stenosis, pulmonary hypertension, pericardial and pleural effusions, orthopnea and dyspnea, with atrial and ventricular arrhythmias and syncope. Coexisting chronic kidney disease and mineral‑bone disturbance suggest cardiorenal involvement.

phenotypes.label: Skin and respiratory tract cancers
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are cutaneous malignancies (melanoma and other skin cancers) alongside primary malignancy of the respiratory tract. These are distinct disease processes without a single unifying clinical theme, and additional scattered conditions (e.g., alopecia, knee synovial cyst) further indicate a mixed cluster.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptom catch-all
phenotypes.description: General acute complaints such as chest or abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, and shortness of breath, alongside common chronic comorbidities like hypertension, GERD, COPD, anxiety/depression, and obesity. This reflects non‑specific presentations rather than a single unifying condition.

phenotypes.label: Bilateral upper eyelid dermatochalasis
phenotypes.description: Dermatochalasis of both upper eyelids dominates, indicating eyelid skin redundancy that can reduce superior visual fields. Associated ocular conditions such as glaucomatous visual field defects, pseudophakia, and secondary cataract, with occasional cutis laxa, support an eyelid/ocular degenerative theme.

phenotypes.label: Obstetric complications and gynecologic disorders
phenotypes.description: Reproductive health pattern with pregnancy states and complications including miscarriage, pre‑eclampsia, premature labor, and high‑risk pregnancy. Concurrent gynecologic conditions include abnormal uterine bleeding, pelvic pain, uterine fibroids, ovarian cysts, cervicovaginal infections (bacterial vaginosis, trichomoniasis), vaginitis, dyspareunia, and abnormal Pap smear.

phenotypes.label: Mixed ear and eye conditions
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated clusters are present: otologic conditions (Eustachian tube dysfunction, otitis externa, mastoiditis) and separate ophthalmic diagnoses (myopia, astigmatism, cataract, posterior vitreous detachment). There is no single clinical theme connecting these groups.

phenotypes.label: Retinal disorders, cataract and glaucoma
phenotypes.description: Ocular degenerative and retinal disease cluster: retinal detachment/tears, vitreous degeneration, macular hole, diabetic retinopathy, and age-related cataracts, with co-occurring glaucoma and dry eye. Refractive errors (myopia, hyperopia, presbyopia) and blepharitis are common adjuncts. Some patients exhibit central scotoma or bilateral blindness, reflecting advanced vision loss.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD, anxiety/depression, chronic pain, obstructive sleep apnea, hypothyroidism, obesity, osteoarthritis, and common pain and dizziness complaints predominate. This reflects a broad chronic multimorbidity profile seen across many patients rather than a single unifying disease process.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptoms and chronic comorbidities
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute complaints such as chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea/vomiting, abdominal and headache presentations appear alongside common chronic conditions including diabetes, hyperlipidemia, hypothyroidism, obesity, depression, glaucoma, and GERD. This reflects a general multimorbidity and symptom burden rather than a single unifying disease process.

phenotypes.label: Foot/ankle enthesopathy and deformity
phenotypes.description: Mechanical foot and ankle pathology centered on enthesopathy, with joint stiffness, localized ankle/foot arthralgia, and acquired foot deformities. The pattern reflects degenerative and overuse changes that impair posture and mobility.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This combines unrelated conditions: allergic asthma; hand tendon disorders such as trigger finger and ganglion cyst; and dermatologic problems including epidermoid/sebaceous cysts, tinea, and facial cellulitis. These conditions do not reflect a single clinical pathway, so this is not useful for defining a specific phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and focal seizure disorders
phenotypes.description: Epilepsy and seizure disorders predominate, especially localization‑related/temporal lobe epilepsy with partial and complex partial seizures, including status epilepticus and refractory disease. Co‑occurring focal neurologic signs (dysarthria, dysmetria, nystagmus, pronator drift, facial asymmetry) and structural brain conditions (hydrocephalus, meningioma, intracranial or subdural hemorrhage, encephalitis, aneurysm) point to focal cortical pathology as cause or comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptom catch-all
phenotypes.description: Non‑specific acute presentations dominate, including pain, chest pain, nausea, shortness of breath, vomiting, diarrhea, wheezing, and abdominal pain, with anxiety and other common complaints. This reflects general symptom coding across many encounters rather than a single unifying disease process.

phenotypes.label: Crohn’s disease with inflammatory arthropathy
phenotypes.description: Crohn’s disease involving small and large bowel, accompanied by inflammatory polyarthropathy and enthesopathies (epicondylitis, Achilles bursitis). Neuropathic pain and paresthesia features (e.g., carpal tunnel findings, trigeminal neuralgia) also appear, with some records noting a positive tuberculin test.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity and acute symptom baseline
phenotypes.description: Broad mix of common cardiometabolic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, coronary disease, heart failure) with nonspecific cardiopulmonary and gastrointestinal symptoms plus anxiety/depression. This reflects a general clinical backdrop seen across many patients rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Mixed musculoskeletal and dermatologic conditions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine two unrelated themes: Achilles/heel pain disorders (Achilles tendinopathy, plantar fasciitis, calcaneal spur, heel pain) and dermatologic diagnoses (acne, rosacea, folliculitis). These involve different organ systems without a single unifying diagnosis, so the grouping lacks a coherent clinical story.

phenotypes.label: Hip osteoarthritis and pain syndromes
phenotypes.description: Hip osteoarthritis with regional pain conditions of the pelvic/hip joint, including trochanteric bursitis, acetabular labral tear, and hip sprain. This reflects chronic degenerative hip pathology with laterality-specific pain and arthropathy, leading to activity-related hip pain and limited mobility in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Sexual dysfunction with menopausal/thyroid disorders
phenotypes.description: Pattern of reduced libido/sexual desire, often in the perimenopausal transition. Endocrine comorbidities are prominent, including thyroid disease (postoperative hypothyroidism, exophthalmos suggestive of Graves) and calcium metabolism disorders, which can contribute to sexual symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are broad category labels and scattered, unrelated findings (endocrine and hematopoietic categories, organizing pneumonia, gout, skin and vocal cord disorders). There is no unifying clinical pattern across these conditions, so this is not a usable clinical cluster.

phenotypes.label: Malignant lymphoma
phenotypes.description: Malignant lymphoma is the dominant condition represented. This reflects patients with hematologic malignancy, often accompanied by related complications such as cytopenias, lymphadenopathy, and infection risk.

phenotypes.label: Pelvic floor and neuromuscular dysfunction
phenotypes.description: Pelvic floor dysfunction occurring with skeletal muscle disorders and impaired mobility. Associated features include abnormal posture, coordination problems, sacroiliac/vertebral column pain, REM sleep behavior disorder, and occasional disorders of iron metabolism.

phenotypes.label: Axial spine pain with radiculopathy
phenotypes.description: Axial musculoskeletal pain centered on degenerative spine disease. Prominent features include chronic neck and low back pain with cervical/lumbar radiculopathy, sciatica, myofascial pain, cervicogenic headache, and sacral/pelvic somatic dysfunction. The pattern reflects spine-related nerve root irritation and widespread myofascial involvement.

phenotypes.label: MDS/MPN overlap and lymphoma
phenotypes.description: Hematologic neoplasms dominate, with myelodysplastic syndromes, myeloproliferative disease such as essential thrombocythemia, and MDS/MPN overlap alongside B‑cell lymphoma. Other listed conditions appear as incidental comorbidities and do not change the primary hematologic theme.

phenotypes.label: Psychogenic pain with mood/personality disorders
phenotypes.description: Episodic mood disorder and personality disorder co-occur with pain disorder with psychological factors, indicating a behavioral health pattern with psychogenic pain/somatic symptom expression. Injury-related pain and foot fractures appear as associated acute events in this group.

phenotypes.label: Ankylosing spondylitis and ocular disease
phenotypes.description: This combines axial spondyloarthritis and related spine disease with ulcerative colitis. It also includes ocular conditions such as glaucoma suspect, borderline intraocular pressure, and macular degeneration. These represent distinct systems without a single unifying clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Insomnia and sleep initiation disorders
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on primary insomnia with difficulty initiating and maintaining sleep, sometimes coded alongside parasomnias. Nocturnal leg cramps and reflux-related esophagitis appear as co-occurring conditions that can worsen nighttime symptoms and sleep continuity.

phenotypes.label: Lumbar radiculopathy and spinal stenosis
phenotypes.description: Degenerative lumbar spine disease with radiculopathy and spinal canal stenosis, often accompanied by disc degeneration and low back/radicular pain. Neurologic and postoperative complications are prominent, including cerebrospinal fluid leak, meninges disorders, spinal cord injury or paralysis, wound issues, bone graft displacement, ileus, and postoperative confusion.

phenotypes.label: Paraplegia with neurogenic bladder and bowel
phenotypes.description: Neurogenic bladder and bowel in the setting of paraplegia/spinal cord dysfunction, with frequent urinary tract infections, pressure ulcers, limb weakness, and spinal deformity. The pattern reflects chronic neurogenic and immobility complications rather than primary dementia features, occasionally accompanied by autonomic tachyarrhythmia and metabolic screening codes.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine inflammatory arthritides (gout, polymyalgia rheumatica, seropositive rheumatoid arthritis) with an unrelated cardiac arrhythmia (atrial flutter). It also includes basal cell carcinoma and neurologic features such as bradykinesia, tremor, and personality change. There is no single unifying clinical mechanism, so this is not a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: HIV with opportunistic infections and ADHD
phenotypes.description: This set blends two unrelated clinical patterns. One centers on HIV disease with opportunistic infections and associated dermatologic/anorectal conditions (e.g., Pneumocystis pneumonia, esophageal candidiasis, anal dysplasia, prurigo/lichen). The other centers on attention‑deficit/hyperactivity disorder and childhood emotional disorders, which are not part of the same clinical pathway.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes with microvascular and PAD complications
phenotypes.description: Pattern of diabetes with established end-organ damage. Features include neuropathy, retinopathy (including proliferative/macular edema), chronic kidney disease, and peripheral arterial disease with foot ulcers, chronic leg wounds, osteomyelitis, and claudication. Cardiometabolic comorbidities such as hypertension and hyperlipidemia are common.

phenotypes.label: Hidradenitis suppurativa with skin infections
phenotypes.description: Chronic inflammatory skin disease centered on hidradenitis suppurativa with concurrent cutaneous herpes infections. Diabetic comorbidity is prominent, with poor glycemic control, hypoglycemic episodes, albuminuria, and retinal hemorrhage suggesting microvascular involvement. Periorbital edema and lid lag appear as associated ocular findings in this group.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptoms and common comorbidities
phenotypes.description: Nonspecific acute complaints (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, gastrointestinal symptoms, headache) appear alongside ubiquitous chronic conditions such as hypertension, chronic kidney disease, and anxiety. This reflects routine presentations and baseline comorbidity coding rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: HIV with male hypogonadism
phenotypes.description: Patients with HIV infection or documented seropositivity, frequently accompanied by male hypogonadism/androgen deficiency. Mucocutaneous infections (herpes simplex, dermatophytosis/onychomycosis) and peripheral neuropathy are noted, with age-related comorbidities such as benign prostatic hyperplasia and thumb carpometacarpal osteoarthritis also present.
